dimensions.note: Change is comment-only plus a version bump: the shared `_lib/retry.ts` twin gains a generated header and drops a stale SYNC note, and model.version aligns to 2026.06.26.0 with a no-op upgrade entry. No credential paths added or altered; the sweep still resolves AWS credentials via environment/profile.
No logging calls changed. The retry helper's onRetry surface and its structured fields are byte-identical below the new comment header.

No control-flow or catch/throw behavior changed. isThrottlingError matching and withRetry full-jitter backoff are byte-identical; only comments differ. The per-(profile,region) scan_error handling in the model is untouched.
Existing retry and metadata/upgrade-chain unit suites stay green; the canonical _lib/ adds its own unit suites run by deno task check. Comment + version-alignment change needs no new tests.
No method semantics changed; the sweep remains read-only (Describe* + GetCallerIdentity). Retry behavior (attempts, base/ceiling, jitter) is unchanged.
No AWS request/response mapping changed. SDK client pins are unchanged (@aws-sdk/* at 3.1073.0); DescribeDBInstances/DescribeReservedDBInstances usage is untouched.
No new files, handles, or network clients. The retry helper still injects deps (random/delay) and opens no resources of its own.

Manifest and model.version aligned at 2026.06.26.0; the version was bumped because the generated comment header in the shipped raw _lib/retry.ts alters the published bundle content hash. No new files added to the package surface; the retry helper's exported symbols (incl. re-exported RetryDeps/RetryEvent) are unchanged.

Mechanical schema/write review: no schema or writeResource payload shape changed. The comment-only retry.ts change and the no-op upgrade entry touch no spec definitions; existing strict instance/reserved/scan_error schemas retained.
Resource lifetime and garbage-collection settings on instance/reserved/scan_error specs unchanged.
No CRUD lifecycle surface; the model is a read-only fan-out sweep. No new lifecycle methods introduced.
Existing check definitions unchanged; the generated header and version bump do not bypass or alter any preflight logic.
Mechanical instance-name review: no writeResource instance-name logic changed (instance/reserved/scan_error key derivation untouched), so resource naming consistency is preserved.
No readResource/dataRepository access patterns changed; the companion report's data dependencies remain the same.

Version bumped 2026.06.22.0 -> 2026.06.26.0 for the bundle-affecting comment header; a matching no-op upgrade entry (toVersion 2026.06.26.0, upgradeAttributes identity) was appended, mirroring the existing no-op chain. swamp upgrades transform stored globalArguments only, never resource artifacts; no resource migration. Runtime behavior is byte-identical.
